race.name: Saskaloppet
race.tagline: Fifty-five kilometers through Saskatchewan's boreal shield — where the cold is absolute, the forest endless, and the snow guaranteed.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple aid stations with warm drinks and food

climate.description: La Ronge at 55°N latitude delivers some of the coldest marathon ski conditions in the world. March temperatures range from -25°C to -5°C. The boreal shield location guarantees deep, dry, cold snow. Wind is less of a factor than pure cold — trees provide shelter but temperatures are relentless.
challenges: Extreme cold — La Ronge is one of the coldest places in southern Canada
Dry, cold snow requires specialized hard wax or zero grip
Remote location limits emergency access on course
Long drive from nearest major city (Saskatoon, 5+ hours)

course.primary: Flat to gently rolling course through boreal forest near La Ronge. The terrain follows northern Saskatchewan's shield landscape — dense spruce forest, frozen lake crossings, and minimal elevation change. Distance, not climbing, is the challenge.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through boreal spruce forest and frozen lake terrain
course.grooming: Groomed by local volunteers — good quality given the remote location
features: Northern boreal forest setting at 55°N latitude
Some of the coldest, most reliable snow conditions for any marathon ski
Cash prizes — rare for a race this size
Frozen lake crossings add unique character
Remote wilderness atmosphere — true backcountry skiing feel

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Guaranteed snow at 55°N latitude is the headline. Flat terrain, tiny field, and extreme remoteness limit the scores. Cash prizes and the adventure factor attract a niche audience. The snow reliability score is perfect.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: TIER 3 — a remote northern adventure with unbeatable snow reliability

history.summary: Founded around 2002, the Saskaloppet brings marathon ski racing to northern Saskatchewan's boreal shield. La Ronge — a small town at 55°N latitude — is an unlikely host for a ski marathon, but its guaranteed cold, deep snow, and vast boreal forest provide ideal conditions. The event offers cash prizes, a rarity among small-field marathons, and has become a destination for skiers seeking authentic northern wilderness skiing.
notable_facts: One of the northernmost marathon ski races in Canada
La Ronge is located on the Canadian Shield, surrounded by boreal forest and lakes
Cash prizes attract competitive skiers despite the small field
Snow conditions are virtually guaranteed — La Ronge averages 6+ months of snow cover
The nearest major city, Saskatoon, is over 5 hours' drive south
Course may include frozen lake crossings depending on conditions
